title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Type-System Trace: Outbox Relay Confirm
summary: An HTTP transaction commits database state then publishes, or publishes then rolls back; a relay deletes outbox rows before confirms; a multi-service workflow treats retries as rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the outbox relay confirm increment in outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Outbox Relay Confirm · Theory Outbox Relay Confirm
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5440 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Outbox Relay Confirm contract for the supplied data.
